513	\subsection{The categorical--module bridge (L1)}
514	\label{sec:cech_l1_bridge}
515	
516	The affine vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} is a statement about the
517	\emph{section} {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
518	\mathcal{F})\) of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} --- a cochain complex of
519	abelian groups --- whereas the combinatorial contracting-homotopy core operates on a
520	concrete complex of localised \(R\)-modules. The passage between the two, the
521	\emph{categorical--module bridge}, decomposes into two self-contained statements: the
522	section identification (each section group is an away localisation, with the
523	restriction maps the canonical localisation maps) and the homology-to-exactness
524	reduction (vanishing of the abstract homology is equivalent to exactness of the
525	underlying localised-module complex). These are isolated as
526	Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} and
527	Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact} below; the assembly --- feeding each
528	positive-degree node through the local-to-global exactness criterion and closing with
529	the dependent combinatorial port --- is carried out in the proof of
530	Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}.

650	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized, lem:section_cech_module_exact,
651	    lem:section_cech_product_equiv, lem:section_cech_objd_apply,
652	    lem:section_cech_coface_match, lem:section_cech_ab_exact}
653	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
654	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (the {\v C}ech complex is
655	  % the complex of localisations) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
656	  % L58--67).
657	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Clearly the {\v C}ech complex
658	  % $\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
659	  % is identified with the complex
660	  % $$
661	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
662	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
663	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
664	  % \ldots
665	  % $$"
666	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
667	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (identification with the
668	  complex of localisations).}
669	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec}
670	  R}\)-module and \(\mathcal{U}\) the standard cover associated with a spanning family
671	  \(s : \iota \to R\). Under the section identification of
672	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}, the section {\v C}ech complex
673	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
674	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} is isomorphic, as a cochain complex, to
675	  the concrete complex of localised \(R\)-modules
676	  \[
677	    D^\bullet : \quad
678	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
679	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0,1\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
680	    \cdots,
681	    \qquad
682	    s_\sigma = \prod_{k} s_{\sigma(k)},
683	  \]
684	  whose differential is the alternating sum of the canonical localisation maps. The
685	  intended top-level conclusion is the directed homology vanishing
686	  \[
687	    1 \leq p
688	    \;\Longrightarrow\;
689	    \operatorname{IsZero}\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
690	      \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{homology}\, p\bigr),
691	  \]
692	  which is the form the affine vanishing
693	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} (\(\operatorname{sectionCech\_affine\_vanishing}\))
694	  consumes; it is read off from positive-degree exactness of the underlying group
695	  homomorphisms,
696	  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\bigl(D^{p-1} \to D^p \to D^{p+1}\bigr)\), which is
697	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}.
698	
699	  The reduction from the abstract homology to this group-level exactness is \emph{not}
700	  a \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}\) statement: the degree-\(p\) object of
701	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) is the \emph{categorical
702	  product}
703	  \(\prod^{c}_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to \iota}
704	  \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) in the category \(\mathrm{Ab}\) of abelian groups (the
705	  alternating-coface-map complex of the \(\mathrm{Ab}\)-valued cosimplicial object
706	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechCosimplicial}\)), not an \(R\)-module object, so the
707	  module-category criterion \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.moduleCat\_exact\_iff}\) does
708	  not apply. The correct chain runs through three steps, isolated as the sub-lemmas
709	  below: \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of the homology is \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) at
710	  \(p\) (\(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\)); \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\)
711	  of a short complex in \(\mathrm{Ab}\) is the underlying-group exactness
712	  \(\forall x_2,\; g(x_2) = 0 \Rightarrow \exists x_1,\; f(x_1) = x_2\) via the abelian
713	  analogue \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff}\)
714	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}); and the categorical product is moved to the
715	  per-\(\sigma\) localised modules by the element-level product equivalence
716	  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\prod^{c} F_\sigma) \simeq \prod_\sigma
717	  \operatorname{ToType}(F_\sigma)\)
718	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}), under which the {\v C}ech coface
719	  differential matches the localisation differential \(\operatorname{dDiff}\) of
720	  \(D^\bullet\) (the abstract cosimplicial unfold
721	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply} followed by the tilde-bridge identification
722	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match}). Transporting the
723	  already-proved exactness \(\operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\) of
724	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact} across the product equivalence then
725	  discharges the group-level exactness. The active target is the tilde case
726	  \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\), for which the per-coordinate comparisons are
727	  immediate from \(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\); a general
728	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) is reduced to this case via the affine equivalence
729	  \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{\Gamma\mathcal{F}}\) (Stacks Tag~01I8), the
730	  separately-deferred globalisation gap recorded in
731	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}.
732	\end{lemma}
733	
734	\begin{proof}
735	  \uses{def:qcoh_sections_localized, def:section_cech_complex}
736	  The term identification is the degreewise statement of
737	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: the degree-\(p\) section group
738	  \(\prod_\sigma \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is \(\prod_\sigma M_{s_\sigma}\), since
739	  each \((p+1)\)-fold intersection \(D(s_{\sigma(0)}) \cap \cdots \cap
740	  D(s_{\sigma(p)})\) is the basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\) of the product
741	  \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\) and the sections there are the away
742	  localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\). The differential identification is item~(5) of
743	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: each face restriction
744	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_{\sigma \circ d_j})) \to \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is the
745	  canonical localisation map \(M_{s_{\sigma \circ d_j}} \to M_{s_\sigma}\), so the
746	  alternating sum of restrictions is the alternating sum of localisation maps, the
747	  differential of \(D^\bullet\) (the coface match of
748	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match}). The homology vanishing then follows in
749	  three steps. First, \(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\) turns
750	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of the homology at \(p\) into \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) of
751	  the short complex at \(p\). Second, because the degree-\(p\) objects are
752	  \emph{categorical products in \(\mathrm{Ab}\)} rather than \(R\)-modules, the abelian
753	  analogue \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff}\)
754	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}) --- not the module-category criterion ---
755	  reduces \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) to the underlying-group exactness
756	  \(\forall x_2,\; g(x_2) = 0 \Rightarrow \exists x_1,\; f(x_1) = x_2\). Third, the
757	  element-level product equivalence
758	  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\prod^{c} F_\sigma) \simeq \prod_\sigma
759	  \operatorname{ToType}(F_\sigma)\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
760	  identifies each product group with the per-\(\sigma\) localised module \(D^p\), so the
761	  group-level exactness is exactly \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}(D^{p-1} \to D^p \to
762	  D^{p+1})\), supplied by \(\operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\)
763	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}).
764	
765	  The identification of the abstract section complex with the concrete localised
766	  complex \(D^\bullet\) --- together with the construction of the prepend transport
767	  \(c\) and the localisation cofaces \(\delta_k\) discharging the unit, shift, and
768	  coface-commutation compatibilities \(\mathtt{hu}\)/\(\mathtt{hsh}\)/\(\mathtt{hcomm}\)
769	  of the dependent combinatorial port --- is the bulk of the bridge. The non-circular
770	  route is reflection along the localisation equivalence on its essential image (the
771	  global-sections functor \(\Gamma\) is the inverse equivalence there, so it reflects
772	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of homology); it is \emph{not} the assertion that
773	  ``\(\Gamma\) preserves homology'', which would presuppose the very affine exactness
774	  being proved.
775	\end{proof}
776	
777	\begin{lemma}
778	
779	[Element-level product equivalence for the section {\v C}ech terms]
780	  \label{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
781	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechProductEquiv,
782	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechProductEquiv_apply,
783	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionProdAddEquiv}
784	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized}
785	  Let \(\{F_\sigma\}_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to \iota}\) be the finite family of
786	  abelian groups \(F_\sigma = \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) appearing in degree \(p\) of
787	  the section {\v C}ech complex of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex}. There is
788	  an equivalence of underlying types
789	  \[
790	    \operatorname{ToType}\Bigl(\prod^{c}_{\sigma} F_\sigma\Bigr)
791	    \;\simeq\;
792	    \prod_{\sigma} \operatorname{ToType}(F_\sigma),
793	  \]
794	  between the elements of the categorical product \(\prod^{c}_{\sigma} F_\sigma\) in
795	  \(\mathrm{Ab}\) and the dependent functions \(\sigma \mapsto\) (element of
796	  \(F_\sigma\)). It is supplied by the Mathlib concrete-limit comparison
797	  \(\operatorname{CategoryTheory.Limits.Concrete.productEquiv}\), which holds because the
798	  forgetful functor \(\operatorname{forget}\,\mathrm{Ab}\) preserves the discrete
799	  product (\(\operatorname{PreservesLimit}(\operatorname{Discrete.functor} F)\)). Under
800	  this equivalence each coordinate
801	  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma)))\) is identified with the away
802	  localisation \(M_{s_\sigma} = \operatorname{dCoeff}\) of
803	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex} by the
804	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) uniqueness of
805	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} (both
806	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) and \(M_{s_\sigma}\) localise \(M\) at \(s_\sigma\), so
807	  the comparison is the canonical isomorphism). This is the move between the
808	  categorical product of \(\mathrm{Ab}\) and the per-\(\sigma\) localised modules.
809	  Its packaging as an additive isomorphism --- the degreewise vertical map of the
810	  ladder transport in Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_ab_exact} --- is recorded as
811	  \(\operatorname{sectionProdAddEquiv}\), the \(\operatorname{AddEquiv}\) underlying
812	  the coordinatewise comparison.
813	  The coordinate projection of the equivalence is recorded separately as the
814	  definitional restatement
815	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv\_apply}\): the \(\sigma\)-component of the
816	  image of \(y\) under the equivalence is the underlying group map of the categorical
817	  projection \(\pi_\sigma\) applied to \(y\).
818	\end{lemma}
819	
820	\begin{proof}
821	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized}
822	  The type-level equivalence is \(\operatorname{Concrete.productEquiv}\) applied to the
823	  family \(\sigma \mapsto F_\sigma\); its hypothesis is that
824	  \(\operatorname{forget}\,\mathrm{Ab}\) preserves the discrete-diagram limit, which is
825	  the standard instance for \(\mathrm{Ab}\). The per-coordinate identification
826	  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))) \cong M_{s_\sigma}\) is the
827	  uniqueness clause of \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\): by
828	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} the restriction map
829	  \(M \to \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) and the localisation unit
830	  \(M \to M_{s_\sigma}\) are both localisations of \(M\) at
831	  \(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers}(s_\sigma)\)
832	  (\(\operatorname{qcohSectionsAwayLocalized}\)), hence canonically isomorphic over
833	  \(M\). Assembling the coordinatewise isomorphisms after the product equivalence gives
834	  the stated identification.
835	\end{proof}
836	
837	\begin{lemma}
838	[Abstract cosimplicial unfold of the section {\v C}ech differential]
839	  \label{lem:section_cech_objd_apply}
840	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCech_objD_apply,
841	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechFaceRestr}
842	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
843	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a presheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and
844	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an open cover. For a multi-index
845	  \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(q+2) \to I\) and a face index
846	  \(i \in \mathrm{Fin}(q+2)\), write
847	  \[
848	    \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i) :
849	      \mathcal{F}\Bigl(\,\bigcap_{l} U_{(\sigma \circ d_i)(l)}\Bigr)
850	      \;\longrightarrow\;
851	      \mathcal{F}\Bigl(\,\bigcap_{k} U_{\sigma(k)}\Bigr)
852	  \]
853	  for the \(i\)-th presheaf face restriction --- the value of \(\mathcal{F}\) on the
854	  inclusion of opens
855	  \(\bigcap_{k} U_{\sigma(k)} \subseteq \bigcap_{l} U_{(\sigma \circ d_i)(l)}\)
856	  obtained from the order-preserving coface \(d_i\) of the simplex category. Then,
857	  read through the product equivalence
858	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}\) of
859	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}, the underlying group action of the
860	  degree-\(q\) differential
861	  \(\operatorname{objD} q\) of the alternating-coface-map complex
862	  \(\operatorname{AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex}
863	  (\operatorname{sectionCechCosimplicial}\,\mathcal{U}\,\mathcal{F})\) is the alternating
864	  sum of the face restrictions applied to the deleted-face coordinates:
865	  \[
866	    \bigl(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}_{q+1}\,(\operatorname{objD} q\, t)\bigr)_\sigma
867	      \;=\;
868	      \sum_{i = 0}^{q+1} (-1)^i \,
869	        \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)
870	          \Bigl(\bigl(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}_{q}\, t\bigr)_{\sigma \circ d_i}\Bigr).
871	  \]
872	  This is \emph{purely cosimplicial}: it records the shape of the section {\v C}ech
873	  differential as an alternating sum of presheaf restrictions, with \emph{no}
874	  sheaf-theoretic or localisation identification of those restrictions. It is the
875	  abstract shape that the localisation differential \(\operatorname{dDiff}\) of
876	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex} matches once the tilde-bridge
877	  identification of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match} is supplied.
878	\end{lemma}
879	
880	\begin{proof}
881	  \uses{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
882	  Unfold the degree-\(q\) map of \(\operatorname{alternatingCofaceMapComplex}\) on
883	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechCosimplicial}\): by construction \(\operatorname{objD} q\)
884	  is the alternating sum \(\sum_{i} (-1)^i\, d^i\) of the cosimplicial cofaces, each
885	  coface \(d^i\) being the \(\operatorname{Pi.lift}\) whose \(\sigma\)-component
886	  projects to the coordinate \(\sigma \circ d_i\) and post-composes with the presheaf
887	  restriction \(\operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\). Reading the
888	  \(\sigma\)-coordinate through the product equivalence (the coordinate-projection
889	  restatement \(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv\_apply}\)) commutes the projection
890	  past the categorical sum: applying the sum of \(\mathrm{Ab}\)-morphisms to an element
891	  distributes over the sum (the finite-sum-application identity
892	  \(\operatorname{ab\_hom\_finsetSum\_apply}\)), and each summand becomes
893	  \((-1)^i\) times the face restriction of the \((\sigma \circ d_i)\)-coordinate. The
894	  per-coordinate projection of the cosimplicial coface is the \(\operatorname{Pi.lift}\)
895	  computation \(\operatorname{Pi.lift\_π}\), which identifies the projected coface with
896	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\) precomposed with the projection to
897	  \(\sigma \circ d_i\). Assembling the signs yields the stated alternating sum.
898	\end{proof}
899	
900	\begin{lemma}
901	
902	[Coface match: the section {\v C}ech differential is the localisation differential]
903	  \label{lem:section_cech_coface_match}
904	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechCofaceMatch,
905	    AlgebraicGeometry.phiL_naturality,
906	    AlgebraicGeometry.phi_naturality}
907	  \uses{lem:section_cech_objd_apply, def:qcoh_sections_localized, def:section_cech_module_complex}
908	  Take \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\), the standard affine cover \(\mathcal{U}\)
909	  associated with a spanning family \(s : \iota \to R\), and \(\mathcal{F} =
910	  \widetilde{M}\) the quasi-coherent sheaf associated with an \(R\)-module \(M\)
911	  (for a general quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\), see the note following the statement). In
912	  the situation of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}, the degree-\(p\)
913	  differential of the section {\v C}ech complex
914	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\), read through the product
915	  equivalence, is exactly the localisation differential \(\operatorname{dDiff}\) of the
916	  module complex \(D^\bullet\) of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex}.
917	  Concretely, under the per-coordinate identifications each section group
918	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is the away localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\) and each face
919	  restriction \(\operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\) is the away-localisation
920	  coface \(\operatorname{dCoface}\), so the alternating sum of face restrictions of
921	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply} becomes the alternating sum of localisation
922	  cofaces, i.e.\ \(\operatorname{dDiff}\).
923	\end{lemma}
924	
925	% NOTE: For the tilde case \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) the per-coordinate
926	% comparison isomorphism and the naturality square follow directly from
927	% \(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\). For a general quasi-coherent
928	% \(\mathcal{F}\), reduce to the tilde case via \(\mathcal{F} \cong
929	% \widetilde{\Gamma\mathcal{F}}\) (Stacks Tag 01I8), the affine equivalence
930	% \(\operatorname{QCoh}(\operatorname{Spec} R) \simeq \operatorname{Mod}(R)\),
931	% which is the open gap recorded in Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}.
932	\begin{proof}
933	  \uses{lem:section_cech_objd_apply, def:section_cech_module_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized}
934	  The proof layers in two steps.
935	
936	  \emph{(i) Abstract unfold.} By Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply}, the
937	  \(\sigma\)-coordinate of the degree-\(p\) differential, read through the product
938	  equivalence, is the alternating sum
939	  \(\sum_{i} (-1)^i\, \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\) of presheaf face
940	  restrictions applied to the deleted-face coordinates. This fixes the shape of the
941	  differential without any sheaf identification of the restrictions.
942	
943	  \emph{(ii) Tilde \(\mathcal{F}\)-bridge.} For \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) each
944	  face restriction is identified with the localisation coface
945	  \(\operatorname{SectionCechModule.dCoface}\) by transporting along a per-coordinate
946	  isomorphism. The section group
947	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) and the localised module
948	  \(M_{s_\sigma} = \operatorname{LocalizedModule}(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers}
949	  s_\sigma)\, M\) both localise \(M\) at the submonoid of powers of
950	  \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\): the former by
951	  \(\operatorname{qcohSectionsAwayLocalized}\) together with the multi-index
952	  intersection identity \(\operatorname{basicOpen\_sprod}\) (which rewrites
953	  \(\bigcap_k D(s_{\sigma(k)})\) as the single basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\)), the latter
954	  by construction. Hence there is a canonical \(R\)-linear isomorphism
955	  \[
956	    \varphi_\sigma :
957	      \operatorname{ToType}\bigl(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\bigr)
958	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
959	      M_{s_\sigma},
960	  \]
961	  the comparison of the two \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) structures, unique by
962	  \(\operatorname{AwayComparison.comparison\_unique}\) and supplied by
963	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule.iso}\). The naturality square
964	  \[
965	    \varphi_\sigma \;\circ\; \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)
966	      \;=\;
967	      \operatorname{dCoface} \;\circ\; \varphi_{\sigma \circ d_i}
968	  \]
969	  is exactly \(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\) of
970	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: the presheaf restriction of
971	  \(\widetilde{M}\) between the two section groups is the away-localisation comparison
972	  of the two localisation units. This face-naturality is recorded in two forms: at the
973	  bare localisation level as \(\operatorname{phiL\_naturality}\) and at the sheaf level
974	  (with \(\varphi_\sigma = \operatorname{phi}\)) as \(\operatorname{phi\_naturality}\).
975	  Substituting (ii) into (i) and summing with signs
976	  identifies the section {\v C}ech differential with \(\operatorname{dDiff}\).
977	
978	  The isomorphism \(\varphi_\sigma\) is obtained by matching the two descriptions of
979	  the sections of \(\widetilde{M}\) over \(D(s_\sigma)\): the cosimplicial functor
980	  provides one description and the localisation property of
981	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} provides the other. Their agreement,
982	  together with the naturality square, is the content of this bridge.
983	\end{proof}
984

986	[Abelian-group exactness criterion for the section {\v C}ech complex]
987	  \label{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}
988	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechAbExact,

3400	\begin{proof}
3401	  \uses{def:standard_affine_cover, lem:affine_faces_mem, lem:primeSpectrum_isBasis_basicOpen}
3402	  \leanok
3403	  The basic opens \(D(g)\), \(g \in R\), form a topological basis of \(\operatorname{Spec} R\)
3404	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:primeSpectrum_isBasis_basicOpen}), so each member \(W_\alpha\) of the given covering
3405	  is a union of basic opens contained in it; choosing one basic open inside each \(W_\alpha\) through
3406	  each point of \(V\) yields a basic-open covering of \(D(f)\) refining \(\{W_\alpha\}\). The
3407	  distinguished open \(D(f)\) is quasi-compact, hence a finite subfamily
3408	  \(D(g_1), \dots, D(g_n)\) already covers \(D(f)\); recording for each \(i\) the index
3409	  \(\varphi(i) \in A\) of a member containing \(D(g_i)\) gives the asserted finite refinement
3410	  \(D(f) = \bigcup_{i=1}^n D(g_i)\) with \(D(g_i) \subseteq W_{\varphi(i)}\). The basis is closed
3411	  under the intersections required by Tag 009L by Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_faces_mem}, so the standard
3412	  covers form a cofinal system in the sense of the sheaf-on-a-basis cofinality principle.
3413	\end{proof}
3414	
3415	\begin{lemma}
3416	[Section surjectivity for the affine cover system]
3417	  \label{lem:affine_surj_of_vanishing}
3418	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.affine_surj_of_vanishing}
3419	  \uses{lem:ses_cech_h1, lem:standard_cover_cofinal, lem:to_sheaf_preserves_epi,
3420	    lem:presheaf_is_locally_surjective, lem:sheaf_locally_surjective_iff_epi,
3421	    lem:scheme_isBasis_affineOpens}
3422	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-ses-cech-h1}
3423	  % (read from references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1592--1605).
3424	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a ringed space.
3425	  % Let
3426	  % $$
3427	  % 0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{H} \to 0
3428	  % $$
3429	  % be a short exact sequence of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules.
3430	  % Let $U \subset X$ be an open subset.
3431	  % If there exists a cofinal system of open coverings $\mathcal{U}$
3432	  % of $U$ such that $\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0$,
3433	  % then the map $\mathcal{G}(U) \to \mathcal{H}(U)$ is
3434	  % surjective."
3435	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-ses-cech-h1}.}
3436	  Let \(V\) be a distinguished open of the affine and let \(S : 0 \to S_1 \to S_2 \to S_3 \to 0\) be a
3437	  short exact sequence of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules whose left term \(S_1\) has vanishing positive
3438	  {\v C}ech cohomology over the standard covers of \(V\), i.e.\ \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, S_1) = 0\)
3439	  for every standard cover \(\mathcal{U}\) of \(V\) and every \(p > 0\). Then the section map
3440	  \(S_2(V) \to S_3(V)\) is surjective. This discharges the \(\mathrm{surj\_of\_vanishing}\) field of
3441	  the affine cover system.
3442	\end{lemma}
3443	\begin{proof}
3444	  \uses{lem:ses_cech_h1, lem:standard_cover_cofinal, lem:to_sheaf_preserves_epi,
3445	    lem:presheaf_is_locally_surjective, lem:sheaf_locally_surjective_iff_epi,
3446	    lem:scheme_isBasis_affineOpens}
3447	  Fix a distinguished open \(V \in \mathcal{B}\) and a section \(t \in S_3(V)\); we produce a global
3448	  lift of \(t\) over \(V\).
3449	
3450	  \emph{Step 1 (local surjectivity of the section map).} The map \(g : S_2 \to S_3\) is an
3451	  epimorphism in the category of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. By the gap-fill
3452	  Lemma~\ref{lem:to_sheaf_preserves_epi} the underlying-abelian-sheaf functor preserves this
3453	  epimorphism, so the induced map of abelian sheaves is an epimorphism; by
3454	  Lemma~\ref{lem:sheaf_locally_surjective_iff_epi} an epimorphism of sheaves is locally surjective,
3455	  hence \(g\) is a locally surjective morphism of presheaves
3456	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:presheaf_is_locally_surjective}).
3457	
3458	  \emph{Step 2 (refine to a standard cover carrying local lifts).} Local surjectivity at \(t\)
3459	  produces an open covering \(\{W_\alpha\}\) of \(V\) together with sections
3460	  \(t_\alpha \in S_2(W_\alpha)\) mapping to \(t|_{W_\alpha}\). Since the affine opens form a basis
3461	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:scheme_isBasis_affineOpens}) and standard covers are cofinal among open coverings
3462	  of \(V\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:standard_cover_cofinal}), refine \(\{W_\alpha\}\) to a standard cover
3463	  \(\mathcal{U} = \{D(g_i)\} \in \mathrm{Cov}\) of \(V\); restricting the \(t_\alpha\) along the
3464	  refinement yields local lifts \(s^{\mathrm{loc}}_i \in S_2(D(g_i))\) with
3465	  \(g(s^{\mathrm{loc}}_i) = t|_{D(g_i)}\).
3466	
3467	  \emph{Step 3 (glue via \(\check{H}^1 = 0\)).} The left term \(S_1\) has vanishing positive
3468	  {\v C}ech cohomology over every covering of \(V\) in \(\mathrm{Cov}\) (the field hypothesis); in
3469	  particular \(\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U}, S_1) = 0\). Feeding the cover \(\mathcal{U}\), the local lifts
3470	  \(s^{\mathrm{loc}}\), and this vanishing to the {\v C}ech \(\check{H}^1\)-surjectivity criterion
3471	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:ses_cech_h1}, which is stated over a raw finite family of opens) produces a global
3472	  section \(s \in S_2(V)\) with \(g(s) = t\). Hence \(S_2(V) \to S_3(V)\) is surjective.
3473	\end{proof}

3475	\begin{lemma}[The sheafification--toSheaf square]
3476	  \label{lem:sheafificationCompToSheaf_mathlib}
3477	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.sheafificationCompToSheaf}
3478	  \mathlibok
3479	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3480	  Let \(R\) be a sheaf of rings for the Grothendieck topology \(J\), and write
3481	  \(L := \operatorname{sheafification}(\mathrm{id}_{R_0})
3482	  : \mathrm{PreMod}(R_0) \to \mathrm{Mod}(R)\) for the sheafification functor from presheaves of
3483	  \(R_0\)-modules to sheaves of \(R\)-modules (a left adjoint and a localization, whose counit is an
3484	  isomorphism). Then there is a natural isomorphism of functors
3485	  \[
3486	    L \,\circ\, \mathrm{toSheaf}\,R \;\cong\;
3487	    \mathrm{toPresheaf}\,R_0 \,\circ\, \operatorname{sheafify}_J^{\mathrm{Ab}},
3488	  \]
3489	  where \(\mathrm{toPresheaf}\,R_0\) forgets a presheaf of modules to its underlying presheaf of
3490	  abelian groups and \(\operatorname{sheafify}_J^{\mathrm{Ab}} = \mathrm{presheafToSheaf}\,J\,
3491	  \mathbf{Ab}\) is sheafification of presheaves of abelian groups. This is the commuting square
3492	  relating module-sheafification to abelian-group-sheafification through the forgetful functors.
3493	\end{lemma}
3494	
3495	\begin{lemma}[Colimit preservation gives epimorphism preservation]
3496	  \label{lem:preservesEpi_of_preservesColimitsOfShape_mathlib}
3497	  \lean{CategoryTheory.preservesEpimorphisms_of_preservesColimitsOfShape}
3498	  \mathlibok
3499	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3500	  Let \(F : \mathcal{C} \to \mathcal{D}\) be a functor that preserves colimits of shape
3501	  \(\mathrm{WalkingSpan}\) (equivalently, preserves pushouts). Then \(F\) preserves epimorphisms: if
3502	  \(f\) is an epimorphism in \(\mathcal{C}\) then \(F(f)\) is an epimorphism in \(\mathcal{D}\). In
3503	  particular a functor preserving finite colimits preserves epimorphisms, since
3504	  \(\mathrm{WalkingSpan}\) is a finite shape.
3505	\end{lemma}
3506	
3507	\begin{lemma}[The underlying-abelian-sheaf functor preserves finite colimits]
3508	  \label{lem:toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits}
3509	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits}
3510	  \uses{lem:sheafificationCompToSheaf_mathlib}
3511	  \textit{Project-bespoke gap-fill (the missing right-exact dual of \(\mathrm{toSheaf}\)).}
3512	  The forgetful functor \(\mathrm{toSheaf}\,R : \mathrm{Mod}(R) \to \mathrm{Sh}(J, \mathbf{Ab})\)
3513	  from sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules to the underlying sheaves of abelian groups preserves
3514	  finite colimits. This is the right-exact counterpart of the limit-side fact that
3515	  \(\mathrm{toSheaf}\,R\) preserves finite limits; Mathlib supplies the latter but not the former, so
3516	  it is built here.
3517	\end{lemma}
3518	\begin{proof}
3519	  \uses{lem:sheafificationCompToSheaf_mathlib}
3520	  Write \(L := \operatorname{sheafification}(\mathrm{id}_{R_0}) : \mathrm{PreMod}(R_0) \to
3521	  \mathrm{Mod}(R)\) for the module-sheafification functor; \(L\) is a left adjoint whose counit is an
3522	  isomorphism, so it is a localization that preserves all colimits.
3523	
3524	  \emph{Step 1 (the composite \(L \circ \mathrm{toSheaf}\) preserves finite colimits).} By the
3525	  square isomorphism (Lemma~\ref{lem:sheafificationCompToSheaf_mathlib}),
3526	  \(L \circ \mathrm{toSheaf}\,R \cong \mathrm{toPresheaf}\,R_0 \circ
3527	  \operatorname{sheafify}_J^{\mathrm{Ab}}\). The forgetful functor \(\mathrm{toPresheaf}\,R_0\) on
3528	  presheaves of modules preserves finite colimits (colimits of presheaves of modules are computed
3529	  objectwise on the underlying abelian groups), and abelian-group sheafification
3530	  \(\operatorname{sheafify}_J^{\mathrm{Ab}}\) is a left adjoint, hence preserves all colimits.
3531	  Therefore their composite, and so the isomorphic functor \(L \circ \mathrm{toSheaf}\,R\), preserves
3532	  finite colimits.
3533	
3534	  \emph{Step 2 (descend to \(\mathrm{toSheaf}\) per diagram).} Fix a finite diagram
3535	  \(F : K \to \mathrm{Mod}(R)\). Since the counit of the sheafification adjunction is an isomorphism,
3536	  \(F \cong (F \circ \mathrm{forget}\,R) \circ L\), where \(\mathrm{forget}\,R\) is the underlying
3537	  presheaf-of-modules functor; consequently the colimit of \(F\) is computed as
3538	  \(\operatorname{colim} F \cong L(\operatorname{colim}(F \circ \mathrm{forget}\,R))\). Applying the
3539	  colimit-preserving composite \(L \circ \mathrm{toSheaf}\,R\) of Step 1 to the colimit cocone of
3540	  \(F \circ \mathrm{forget}\,R\) shows that \(\mathrm{toSheaf}\,R\) carries the colimit cocone of
3541	  \(F\) to a colimit cocone. As \(K\) was an arbitrary finite diagram, \(\mathrm{toSheaf}\,R\)
3542	  preserves finite colimits.
3543	\end{proof}
3544	
3545	\begin{lemma}
3546	[The underlying-abelian-sheaf functor preserves epimorphisms]
3547	  \label{lem:to_sheaf_preserves_epi}
3548	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms}
3549	  \uses{lem:toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits, lem:preservesEpi_of_preservesColimitsOfShape_mathlib}
3550	  \textit{Project-bespoke gap-fill (instance).}
3551	  The forgetful functor from sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules to the underlying sheaves of
3552	  abelian groups preserves epimorphisms: if \(g : S_2 \to S_3\) is an epimorphism of
3553	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules then the induced morphism of underlying abelian sheaves is an
3554	  epimorphism. This is the one small instance unlocking the passage from a module epimorphism to
3555	  local surjectivity in Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_surj_of_vanishing}.
3556	\end{lemma}
3557	\begin{proof}
3558	  \uses{lem:toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits, lem:preservesEpi_of_preservesColimitsOfShape_mathlib}
3559	  The functor \(\mathrm{toSheaf}\,R\) preserves finite colimits
3560	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits}); in particular it preserves colimits of shape
3561	  \(\mathrm{WalkingSpan}\), which is a finite shape. A functor that preserves pushouts preserves
3562	  epimorphisms (Lemma~\ref{lem:preservesEpi_of_preservesColimitsOfShape_mathlib}), so
3563	  \(\mathrm{toSheaf}\,R\) carries the epimorphism \(g\) to an epimorphism of abelian sheaves.
3564	\end{proof}
3565	
3566	\begin{lemma}[Locally surjective morphisms of presheaves]
3567	  \label{lem:presheaf_is_locally_surjective}
3568	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective}
3569	  \mathlibok
3570	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3571	  For a Grothendieck topology \(J\), a morphism \(\alpha : \mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{H}\) of presheaves
3572	  is \emph{locally surjective} when every section of \(\mathcal{H}\) over an object is, locally on a
3573	  covering sieve, in the image of \(\alpha\). On a topological space this is the predicate
3574	  \(\operatorname{TopCat.Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective}\): for each open \(V\) and each section
3575	  \(t \in \mathcal{H}(V)\) there is an open covering \(\{W_\alpha\}\) of \(V\) with \(t|_{W_\alpha}\)
3576	  in the image of \(\alpha_{W_\alpha}\).
3577	\end{lemma}
3578	
3579	\begin{lemma}[An epimorphism of sheaves is locally surjective]
3580	  \label{lem:sheaf_locally_surjective_iff_epi}
3581	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Sheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff_epi'}
3582	  \mathlibok
3583	  \uses{lem:presheaf_is_locally_surjective}
3584	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3585	  A morphism of sheaves (of abelian groups, valued in a suitable category) is an epimorphism in the
3586	  category of sheaves if and only if it is locally surjective. In particular an epimorphism of
3587	  abelian sheaves on the space \(X\) is locally surjective, supplying the covering with local lifts
3588	  used in Step 2 of Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_surj_of_vanishing}.
3589	\end{lemma}
3590	
3591	\begin{lemma}[Affine opens form a basis]
3592	  \label{lem:scheme_isBasis_affineOpens}
3593	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isBasis_affineOpens}
3594	  \mathlibok
3595	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3596	  The affine opens of a scheme \(X\) form a basis for its topology. On \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) this
3597	  refines any open covering of a distinguished open to one by basic/affine opens, the input that
3598	  turns a local-surjectivity covering into a standard cover lying in \(\mathrm{Cov}\)
3599	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:standard_cover_cofinal}).
3600	\end{lemma}
3601	
3602	\begin{lemma}[Basic opens form a topological basis of the prime spectrum]
3603	  \label{lem:primeSpectrum_isBasis_basicOpen}
3604	  \lean{PrimeSpectrum.isTopologicalBasis_basic_opens}
3605	  \mathlibok
3606	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3607	  For a commutative (semi)ring \(R\), the distinguished/basic opens \(D(r)\), \(r \in R\), form a
3608	  topological basis of \(\operatorname{Spec} R\): the sets \(\{D(r) : r \in R\}\) are open and every
3609	  open of \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) is a union of them. This is the basis input used to refine an
3610	  arbitrary open covering of a distinguished open to one by basic opens
3611	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:standard_cover_cofinal}).
3612	\end{lemma}

3614	\begin{lemma}
3615	
3616	\leanok
3617	[Standard-cover opens are the distinguished opens \(D(s_i)\)]
3618	  \label{lem:cover_datum_bridge}
3619	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coverOpen_affineOpenCoverOfSpan}
3620	  \uses{def:standard_affine_cover}
3621	  % NOTE: This lemma provides the open-level identity coverOpen 𝒰 i = D(s_i) for a standard
3622	  % affine cover; it is used to recognise a standard cover's opens as distinguished opens
3623	  % (e.g. to relate a covering of D(f) to its standard opens). The injective acyclicity
3624	  % condition of the affine cover system is discharged directly by the family-form
3625	  % Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic}, not via this lemma.
3626	  \textit{Project-bespoke compatibility lemma.}
3627	  Let \(\mathcal{U}\) be the standard affine cover associated to a spanning family
3628	  \(s : \iota \to R\) with \(\operatorname{span}(\operatorname{range} s) = \top\)
3629	  (Definition~\ref{def:standard_affine_cover}). For each index \(i\) the \(i\)-th open of
3630	  \(\mathcal{U}\) is exactly the distinguished open \(D(s_i)\):
3631	  \[
3632	    \operatorname{coverOpen} \mathcal{U}\, i \;=\; D(s_i)
3633	    \quad\text{in } \mathrm{Opens}(\operatorname{Spec} R).
3634	  \]
3635	  This identifies the opens of a standard cover with distinguished opens, which is the form in
3636	  which a standard cover is recognised inside the basis \(\mathcal{B}\) of the affine cover system.
3637	\end{lemma}
3638	\begin{proof}
3639	  \uses{def:standard_affine_cover}
3640	  \leanok
3641	  By construction the \(i\)-th member of the standard affine cover attached to \(s\) is the basic
3642	  open \(D(s_i)\) of \(\operatorname{Spec} R\); the spanning hypothesis
3643	  \(\operatorname{span}(\operatorname{range} s) = \top\) guarantees these distinguished opens cover
3644	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R\), and the open carried at index \(i\) is \(D(s_i)\) on the nose.
3645	\end{proof}

3647	\begin{lemma}
3648	
3649	\leanok
3650	[Injective acyclicity for the affine cover system]
3651	  \label{lem:affine_injective_acyclic}
3652	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.affine_injective_acyclic}
3653	  \uses{lem:injective_cech_acyclic, lem:cover_datum_bridge}
3654	  % NOTE: This covers only standard covers of the whole affine (spanning hypothesis
3655	  % span(range s) = ⊤); the injective acyclicity condition of the affine cover system is
3656	  % discharged by the family-form Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic}, which applies to
3657	  % any distinguished open D(f), not just the whole affine.
3658	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-injective-trivial-cech}
3659	  % (read from references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1407--1422).
3660	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a ringed space.
3661	  % Let $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i$ be an open covering.
3662	  % Let $\mathcal{I}$ be an injective $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
3663	  % Then
3664	  % $$
3665	  % \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) =
3666	  % \left\{
3667	  % \begin{matrix}
3668	  % \mathcal{I}(U) & \text{if} & p = 0 \\
3669	  % 0 & \text{if} & p > 0
3670	  % \end{matrix}
3671	  % \right.
3672	  % $$"
3673	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-injective-trivial-cech}.}
3674	  Every injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{I}\) has vanishing positive-degree {\v C}ech
3675	  cohomology over a standard cover \emph{of the whole affine}: if
3676	  \(s : \iota \to R\) spans, \(\operatorname{span}(\operatorname{range} s) = \top\), so that the
3677	  distinguished opens \(D(s_i)\) cover \(\operatorname{Spec} R\), then
3678	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for every \(p > 0\). This is the special case of the
3679	  family-form injective {\v C}ech-acyclicity (Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic}) at the
3680	  spanning family \(s\); the general \(\mathrm{injective\_acyclic}\) field of the affine cover system
3681	  is discharged by that family-form lemma directly.
3682	\end{lemma}
3683	\begin{proof}
3684	  \uses{lem:injective_cech_acyclic, lem:cover_datum_bridge}
3685	  \leanok
3686	  The opens of the standard cover \(\mathcal{U}\) attached to the spanning family \(s\) are the
3687	  distinguished opens \(D(s_i)\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:cover_datum_bridge}). Applying the family-form
3688	  injective {\v C}ech-acyclicity (Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic}) to the finite family
3689	  \(i \mapsto D(s_i)\) gives \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\).
3690	\end{proof}

3692	\begin{definition}
3693	[The affine cover system]
3694	  \label{def:affine_cover_system}
3695	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.affineCoverSystem}
3696	  \uses{def:basis_cov_system, lem:affine_faces_mem, lem:affine_surj_of_vanishing,
3697	    lem:injective_cech_acyclic}
3698	  % NOTE: The injective acyclicity field is discharged by Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic}
3699	  % (family-form, no covering hypothesis, applies to standard covers of any D(f));
3700	  % the surj_of_vanishing field by Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_surj_of_vanishing}.
3701	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KG, Lemma
3702	  % \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} (proof, choice of basis and
3703	  % coverings) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L160--163).
3704	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "As our basis $\mathcal{B}$ for the topology of $X$ we are going to use
3705	  % the affine opens of $X$.
3706	  % As our set $\text{Cov}$ of open coverings we are going to use the standard
3707	  % open coverings of affine opens of $X$."
3708	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KG
3709	  (\texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero}, proof).}
3710	  The \emph{affine cover system} for an affine scheme \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\) (or an affine open
3711	  of a scheme) is the instance of Definition~\ref{def:basis_cov_system} whose basis \(\mathcal{B}\) is
3712	  the distinguished opens \(D(f)\), \(f \in R\), and whose admissible coverings \(\mathrm{Cov}\) are
3713	  the standard open covers (finite coverings of a distinguished open by distinguished opens). Beyond
3714	  the basis \(\mathcal{B}\) and the covering set \(\mathrm{Cov}\) just described, it carries three
3715	  proof fields: the \(\mathrm{faces\_mem}\) field by Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_faces_mem}; the
3716	  \(\mathrm{surj\_of\_vanishing}\) field by Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_surj_of_vanishing}; and the
3717	  \(\mathrm{injective\_acyclic}\) field by the family-form injective {\v C}ech-acyclicity
3718	  Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} (applied to the distinguished opens of each standard cover).
3719	\end{definition}

3738	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $R$ be a ring. Let $M$ be an $R$-module. Let $\widetilde M$

3750	  Let \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\) be an affine scheme and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
3751	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Writing \(M = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})\) for its module of global sections,

3754	  \(\mathcal{F}\) over a distinguished open \(D(f)\) are the away localisation \(M_f\). This is the
3755	  affine quasi-coherent structure theorem (Tag 01HV, with the equivalence quasi-coherent-on-affine
3756	  \(\simeq\) \(R\)-modules, Tag 01I8).

3758	\begin{proof}
3759	  \uses{def:standard_affine_cover, lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation}
3760	  On an affine scheme the global-sections functor \(\mathcal{F} \mapsto \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})\) and
3761	  the tilde functor \(M \mapsto \widetilde{M}\) are mutually inverse equivalences between
3762	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and \(R\)-modules; the counit
3763	  \(\widetilde{\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})} \to \mathcal{F}\) is an isomorphism for quasi-coherent
3764	  \(\mathcal{F}\). By Tag 01HV the sections of \(\widetilde{M}\) over \(D(f)\) are \(M_f\), so the
3765	  isomorphism identifies \(\Gamma(D(f), \mathcal{F})\) with \(M_f\).

3768	  \(\operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma} : \widetilde{\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})} \to \mathcal{F}\) is known to
3769	  be an isomorphism, the asserted iso \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})}\) is its
3770	  inverse (the one-line conditional argument formalized). The hypothesis that the counit is an
3771	  isomorphism holds for any \(\mathcal{F}\) admitting a global presentation
3772	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation}), and unconditionally for quasi-coherent
3773	  \(\mathcal{F}\) once the 01I8 instance \([{\,}\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}\,\mathcal{F}{\,}] \Rightarrow
3774	  \operatorname{IsIso}(\operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma})\) is supplied (see the decomposition remark
3775	  below).

3849	  The unconditional quasi-coherent statement
3850	  \([{\,}\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}\,\mathcal{F}{\,}] \Rightarrow \mathcal{F} \cong
3851	  \widetilde{\Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})}\) reduces, through
3852	  Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections}, to the single instance
3853	  \([{\,}\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}\,\mathcal{F}{\,}] \Rightarrow
3854	  \operatorname{IsIso}(\operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma})\) on the affine base \(\operatorname{Spec} R\).
3855	  This is the content of Stacks Tag 01I8, organised here as a three-step route into the
3856	  presentation-form discharge:
3857	  \begin{enumerate}
3858	  \item \emph{Global generation.} A quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) on \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) is
3859	    generated by its global sections: one produces a global epimorphism
3860	    \(\mathcal{O}_X^{(I)} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\). Quasi-coherence gives generators only
3861	    locally on a basic-open cover; these are globalised using the quasi-compactness of
3862	    \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) and the localisation-of-sections property of quasi-coherent sheaves
3863	    (sections over \(D(f)\) are the away localisation), the affine global-generation statement of
3864	    Tag 01I8. This is realised by the finite basic-open subcover
3865	    (Lemma~\ref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover}, P0), the localised-sections core
3866	    (Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_localized_sections}, P1), and their assembly into a global generating
3867	    family (Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_global_generation}, P2).
3868	  \item \emph{Presentation.} The kernel of \(\mathcal{O}_X^{(I)} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) is
3869	    again quasi-coherent on \(\operatorname{Spec} R\), hence again globally generated by step (1);
3870	    composing yields a global presentation \(\mathcal{F}.\mathrm{Presentation}\). The kernel's
3871	    quasi-coherence uses the exactness of \(\widetilde{(-)}\)
3872	    (Lemma~\ref{lem:tilde_preserves_kernels}) to land at Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_kernel_qcoh} (P3); the
3873	    two generating families \(\sigma, \tau\) are assembled into the counit-iso input by
3874	    Lemma~\ref{lem:isIso_fromTildeGamma_of_genSections}.
3875	  \item \emph{Counit iso.} Feeding that presentation to
3876	    Lemma~\ref{lem:isIso_fromTildeGamma_of_presentation} makes
3877	    \(\operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma}\) an isomorphism; equivalently, this is the discharge of
3878	    Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation}. Packaged as the unconditional
3879	    instance, this is Lemma~\ref{lem:isIso_fromTildeGamma_of_quasicoherent} (P4), which upgrades
3880	    Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections} to its unconditional quasi-coherent form through
3881	    Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_genSections}.
3882	  \end{enumerate}
3883	  Step (1) is the load-bearing affine global-generation input; steps (2)--(3) are formal. The same
3884	  route is recorded as the route to the affine equivalence \(\operatorname{QCoh}(\operatorname{Spec}
3885	  R) \simeq \operatorname{Mod}(R)\) (Tag 01I8, \texttt{lemma-equivalence-quasi-coherent}).
3886	
3887	  The chain deliberately takes this \emph{global-generation} route (Hartshorne~II.5.14--17, Stacks
3888	  Tag 01I8) rather than the alternative \emph{module-gluing} route suggested by the Stacks proof
3889	  above (which constructs \(M\) by gluing the local modules \(M_i\) along the cocycle
3890	  \(\psi_{ij}\), Algebra \texttt{lemma-glue-modules}). The module-gluing route is not pursued here:
3891	  it requires gluing data of modules and the effective faithfully-flat descent equivalence, neither
3892	  of which is available in the ambient library, whereas every step of the global-generation route
3893	  reduces to ordinary commutative-algebra and sheaf statements (localisation of sections, finite
3894	  quasi-compact patching, locality of kernels) that land directly on the presentation-form discharge
3895	  Lemma~\ref{lem:isIso_fromTildeGamma_of_presentation}. The named blocks
3896	  Lemma~\ref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover} (P0),
3897	  Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_localized_sections} (P1),

3899	  Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_kernel_qcoh} (P3), and
3900	  Lemma~\ref{lem:isIso_fromTildeGamma_of_quasicoherent} (P4)
3901	  below carry out the route P0--P4.
3902	\end{remark}

4238	\begin{proof}
4239	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} (item~(7) and
4240	  proof), Tag 01HV.}
4241	  Kernels in the category of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules are computed
4242	  objectwise and sheafified, and isomorphisms of sheaves may be checked on stalks: a
4243	  morphism of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is an isomorphism if and only if it induces an
4244	  isomorphism on every stalk. So it suffices to compare stalks. By the stalk computation
4245	  for the tilde functor (\texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} item~(7)) the stalk of
4246	  \(\widetilde{M}\) at a point \(x \in \operatorname{Spec} R\) corresponding to the prime
4247	  \(\mathfrak{p}\) is the localisation
4248	  \(\widetilde{M}_x = M_{\mathfrak{p}}\), naturally in \(M\): the stalk functor at \(x\)
4249	  applied to \(\widetilde{(-)}\) is the localisation functor \(M \mapsto M_{\mathfrak{p}}\).
4250	
4251	  Now let \(\varphi : M \to N\) be an \(R\)-module map with kernel
4252	  \(K = \operatorname{Ker}(\varphi)\), giving the exact sequence
4253	  \(0 \to K \to M \xrightarrow{\varphi} N\). Localisation \(R \to R_{\mathfrak{p}}\) is
4254	  flat, hence the functor \(M \mapsto M_{\mathfrak{p}}\) is exact and in particular
4255	  preserves kernels: \(K_{\mathfrak{p}} = \operatorname{Ker}(\varphi_{\mathfrak{p}} :
4256	  M_{\mathfrak{p}} \to N_{\mathfrak{p}})\). Translating through the stalk identification,
4257	  the natural comparison map
4258	  \(\widetilde{K} \to \operatorname{Ker}(\widetilde{\varphi})\) induces, at every point
4259	  \(x \leftrightarrow \mathfrak{p}\), the isomorphism \(K_{\mathfrak{p}} \xrightarrow{\sim}
4260	  \operatorname{Ker}(\varphi_{\mathfrak{p}})\) (using that the stalk functor, being a
4261	  filtered colimit, is itself exact and commutes with forming the kernel). Being a
4262	  stalkwise isomorphism, the comparison map is an isomorphism of sheaves, so

vanishing; the bridge lifts it to affine sheaf vanishing without ever using affine vanishing.

`H^p(U, F) := Ext^p_{X.Modules}(jShriekOU U, F)` where `jShriekOU U := sheafify(free(yoneda U))`

already-shipped `freeYonedaHomEquiv` + `sheafificationAdjunction` (~50–80 LOC). The three facts

`Ext.eq_zero_of_injective` (injective `I` as the 2nd arg — no restriction taken), covariant LES
`Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁/₂/₃` at fixed 1st arg `jShriekOU U`, and `H⁰ ≅ Γ(U,F)` via the
corepresentability chain. The discarded alternatives: Form A `Ext(O_U, F|_U)` needs
restriction-preserves-injectives (≈ a 200–500 LOC `j_!` functor); `Functor.rightDerived` has
free injective vanishing but no packaged LES; `Sheaf.H` is absent. Reversal signal: Ext
universe/smallness pain over `SheafOfModules` → fall back to Route γ (Čech colimit), never `Sheaf.H`.

input.query: SheafOfModules toSheaf preserves finite colimits or creates colimits

content:
Interesting findings:

1. `SheafOfModules.instHasColimitsOfShapeOfPresheafOfModulesValRingCat`: Colimits in `SheafOfModules R` exist when they exist in `PresheafOfModules R.val`. This is how colimits are computed — via presheaves of modules! This confirms the argument that the colimits in module sheaves are "computed via" presheaves.

2. `PresheafOfModules.instPreservesFiniteLimitsSheafOfModulesSheafification`: The sheafification functor for presheaves of modules preserves **finite limits** (not colimits!). 

So the key insight is that `toSheaf` should preserve colimits precisely because colimits in `SheafOfModules R` are constructed by sheafifying the presheaf colimit. I need to find whether there's already a lemma stating that `toSheaf` preserves colimits, or if I need to construct the proof using this sheafification mechanism.

\text{Presheaf}\, \text{Ab} @>{\text{presheafToSheaf}\, J}>> \text{Sheaf}\, \text{Ab} @>{\text{sheafToPresheaf}\, J}>> \text{Presheaf}\, \text{Ab}

# Blueprint Review Report — iter-033

**Reviewer:** blueprint-reviewer subagent  
**Date:** 2026-06-07  
**Directive:** `.archon/logs/iter-033/blueprint-reviewer-iter033-directive.md`

---

## Per-Chapter Checklist

All sections outside the single must-fix block are complete and correct. Known-deferred blocks (P1a geometry gap, protected sorries) are honestly marked and do not contribute to this verdict.

---

## Gate Decisions

### Gate 1 — `AffineSerreVanishing.lean` prover lane

#### `lem:toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits`
**`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits}`** (NEW this iter)

| Field | Status |
|-------|--------|
| complete | **partial** |
| correct | **partial** |

**Findings:**

**[must-fix-this-iter] Step 2 of proof is mathematically correct but unreadable as written.**

The proof has two steps:
- Step 1: `(sheafification α).comp (SheafOfModules.toSheaf R) ≅ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R₀).comp (presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat)` (from `\lean{PresheafOfModules.sheafificationCompToSheaf}`, mathlibok), and this composite preserves finite colimits since both factors do (`toPresheaf_preservesFiniteColimits` on the presheaf side, `presheafToSheaf` as a left adjoint). This step is correct and clearly stated.
- Step 2: Descent to `SheafOfModules.toSheaf R` alone via the reflective-subcategory / counit-is-iso argument. The mathematical argument is: the sheafification functor `sheafification α : SheafOfModules R₀ → Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat` is an equivalence on its image (counit is an isomorphism), so if `(sheafification α) ∘ (toSheaf R)` preserves finite colimits and `sheafification α` is an equivalence, then `toSheaf R` preserves finite colimits. This is sound. However:
  - The current proof prose uses inconsistent composition-order notation, switching between `F ∘ G` and `G.comp F` convention mid-paragraph with at least one apparent typo (`F ∘ forget R` vs `forget R ∘ F`).
  - The key equation that makes Step 2 work — `toSheaf R ≅ (sheafification α)⁻¹ ∘ (sheafification α ∘ toSheaf R)` together with the counit-iso fact — is never stated explicitly, making the descent opaque.

**[must-fix-this-iter] Missing `\uses{}` edge.**  
Step 2 invokes the sheafification adjunction and the fact that the counit `L ∘ ι → id` is an isomorphism on sheaves (i.e., sheafification of a sheaf is itself). This should be covered by `\uses{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}` (or whichever blueprint lemma states the sheafification adjunction counit-iso). That lemma is currently absent from the `\uses{}` list for `lem:toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits`, meaning the dependency graph is incomplete and the block cannot be marked complete.

**Additional note (from Mathlib search):** Mathlib has `SheafOfModules.instPreservesFiniteLimitsSheafAddCommGrpCatToSheaf` (finite **limits** for `toSheaf`), confirming there is no Mathlib-backed finite **colimits** instance — the project-to-build claim is correctly set up. The proof route via `sheafificationCompToSheaf` is the right approach; it just needs Step 2 rewritten clearly.

**Prescribed fix (fast-path):** Writer rewrites Step 2 prose to state explicitly: (a) the counit `(sheafification α) ∘ ι ≅ id` where `ι : Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat → SheafOfModules R₀` is the forgetful direction; (b) therefore `toSheaf R` is a retract of `(sheafification α) ∘ (toSheaf R)` up to natural isomorphism; (c) a retract of a colimit-preserving functor preserves colimits. Notation should be unified (pick one of Lean-style `.comp` or ∘ notation and stick with it). Add `\uses{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}` (verify the label name matches the blueprint lemma for the sheafification adjunction).

---

#### `lem:to_sheaf_preserves_epi`
**`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms}`**

| Field | Status |
|-------|--------|
| complete | **true** (conditional) |

Proof correctly derives epi preservation from `lem:toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits` + Mathlib's `preservesEpimorphisms_of_preservesColimitsOfShape` (pushouts / WalkingSpan). `\uses{}` is correctly set. Conditioned on the above must-fix: once Step 2 of `lem:toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits` is fixed, this block is fully clean.

---

#### `lem:affine_surj_of_vanishing`

|-------|--------|

| correct | **true** |

Three-step proof (local surjectivity → cofinal-cover refinement → H¹=0 gluing via `ses_cech_h1`) is detailed and correct. `\uses{}` edges are properly populated. No issues.

---

#### `def:affine_cover_system`

| Field | Status |
|-------|--------|
| complete | **true** |
| correct | **true** |

Correctly assembles basis = distinguished opens, Cov = standard affine covers, three proof fields corresponding to `BasisCovSystem`. All referenced lemmas are `\leanok` or `\mathlibok`. No issues.

---

**Gate 1 verdict: DOES NOT CLEAR.**

`AffineSerreVanishing.lean` prover should NOT be dispatched this iter. The must-fix in `lem:toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits` (Step 2 prose + missing `\uses{}`) must be resolved first. **Fast-path:** writer applies the prescribed fix above (Step 2 rewrite + `\uses{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}`); re-review is quick since only that one block changed. The other three Gate 1 blocks are already clean.

---

### Gate 2 — `TildeExactness.lean` prover lane (new file to scaffold)

#### `lem:tilde_preserves_kernels`
**`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.tildePreservesFiniteLimits}`**  
(informal proof written iter-032; NOT yet `\leanok`)

| Field | Status |
|-------|--------|
| complete | **true** |
| correct | **true** |
| must-fix findings | none |

The stalkwise-flatness proof is sufficiently detailed and all Mathlib ingredients are verified to exist:

1. **Stalk identity:** `ModuleCat.Tilde.stalkIso` (Mathlib, `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde`) gives `(~M)_𝔭 ≅ M_𝔭` as an R_𝔭-module. ✓
2. **Flatness of localization:** R_𝔭 is flat over R for every prime 𝔭 (standard Mathlib algebra). This means the stalk functor at 𝔭 is exact (preserves finite limits and colimits). ✓
3. **Stalkwise exactness → global exactness:** `TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (Mathlib, `Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.Stalks`) gives the stalkwise iso criterion: a map of sheaves is an iso iff it is stalkwise an iso. Kernels in `Sheaf J Ab` are computed as sheafifications of pointwise kernels, but stalkwise the sheafification doesn't change stalks, so a map that is stalkwise a kernel is globally a kernel. ✓
4. **Conclusion:** For a finite-limit diagram in `SheafOfModules R`, the canonical map `~(lim F) → lim (~F)` is stalkwise an iso (at each 𝔭, it reduces to the iso `(lim F)_𝔭 ≅ lim(F_𝔭)` which holds by flatness + stalkIso), hence globally an iso. ✓

The proof outline gives the prover the exact Mathlib lemma names for each step. Formalization effort is moderate (the main work is chasing the stalkwise-iso criterion through the sheaf-of-modules vs. sheaf-of-abelian-groups layers, which is mechanical given the cited lemmas).

**Gate 2 verdict: CLEARS.**  
`TildeExactness.lean` prover can be dispatched now. The scaffold should contain `tildePreservesFiniteLimits` with proof strategy: introduce `stalkIso`, apply flatness, invoke `isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso`.
